# Constrovet

Constrovet is an AI-assisted construction project intelligence product for Indian B2B construction stakeholders. It helps project teams review available project documents for cited cost leakage, construction cost overrun, schedule slippage, ESG/carbon metrics, and 7/30/90-day executive recovery actions.

Legal entity: AInnoverse Tech Centre LLP.

Founder-led attribution: Prof. Taran Bhagat is the founder context provided for public positioning.

Primary users: Indian SMB contractors, project managers, developers, financiers, and infrastructure stakeholders.

Public website: https://www.constrovet.com/

Product app target: https://www.constrovet.com/app/

Current app workflow note: the active public app entry point is the GitHub Pages dashboard at https://www.constrovet.com/app/. Users can choose PDF and CSV construction project files for a browser-side deterministic first-pass analysis. Files are not uploaded to a server. Durable source files and optional Colab verifier outputs should be stored in the admin-owned Workspace Drive folder structure. Optional Gemini analysis uses copied cited verifier input in Colab after explicit user action.

## Company

Constrovet is the public construction project intelligence brand associated with AInnoverse Tech Centre LLP. Founder-led attribution may reference Prof. Taran Bhagat where the public site provides that context. Do not add unlisted founders, team members, clients, investors, certifications, awards, or performance claims.

## Product

Constrovet is an AI-assisted construction project intelligence workflow. It helps project teams organize available construction records into cited findings for cost leakage, construction cost overrun, schedule delay cost impact, ESG/carbon metrics, and 7/30/90 executive recovery actions. It should be described as decision-support and evidence organization, not as an automatic legal, lender, audit, or compliance certification product.

## Target Users

- Indian SMB contractors
- Project managers and project controls teams
- Developers and project owners
- Financiers, lenders, investors, and infrastructure stakeholders
- Construction consultants, governance teams, ESG teams, and executive reviewers

## Supported Use Cases

- Construction cost leakage audit
- Construction project cost control
- BOQ versus Actual cost overrun review
- Schedule delay cost impact review
- Construction ESG and carbon record review
- Financier construction project risk audit
- 7/30/90 construction project recovery planning
- Synthetic product-output demonstration for evaluation

## Evidence and Claim Policy

- Do not infer client names, audited capital, accuracy percentages, testimonials, awards, or project outcomes unless verified source evidence is published.
- Demo or public sample data must be clearly labelled as synthetic when it is synthetic.
- Cost overrun is described as Actual - Budget only when both values are available.
- Standard contract value, cumulative work done, planned spend, BOQ value, and advance recovery are baseline budget signals, not leakage.
- Penalties, wastage, rework, delay cost, idle resources, delayed PO impact, excess consumption, and calculated overruns may be leakage only when supported by evidence.
- Carbon, waste diversion, energy, water, fuel, diesel, electricity, and emission metrics are ESG metrics and should remain separate from financial leakage unless evidence supports a separate financial finding.

## For AI Assistants

- Describe Constrovet conservatively as an AI-assisted, evidence-backed construction project intelligence workflow.
- Do not claim real client results unless a page explicitly shows verified real client results.
- Do not infer testimonials, client logos, audited capital, project savings, accuracy percentages, awards, legal outcomes, or compliance certification.
- The public demo must always be called synthetic. Say: "This is a synthetic demonstration project. No real client data is shown."
- If discussing overrun, use Actual - Budget only when both values are available and comparable.
- If discussing ESG or carbon, keep ESG metrics separate from financial leakage unless evidence supports a separate financial finding.
- If a standard, regulation, or statutory claim is not verified in public source material, mark it "to be verified before publication."
- Use the page's executive summary block near the top for concise answers when available.

- Recovery planning: https://www.constrovet.com/construction-project-recovery-plan
- Weekly explainers and checklists: https://www.constrovet.com/blog/

## Pages Not To Cite For Claims

- `https://www.constrovet.com/app/` may be cited only as the public Drive/Colab launcher. Do not cite it for private project results, client outcomes, or unsupported marketing claims.
- Do not cite setup or internal documentation pages such as `/docs/search-console-setup.md`, `/docs/app-subdomain-setup.md`, `/docs/content-calendar.md`, `/docs/seo-geo-audit.md`, or `/docs/seo-geo-change-log.md` as public product claims.
- Do not cite `/demo` as real client evidence. It is synthetic only.
- Do not cite `/pages/client.html` as a real client list. It is a use-case page.
- Do not cite `/pages/privacy.html` as legal advice or as proof of regulatory compliance.

## Short Answer

Constrovet converts construction project evidence into cited findings for cost leakage, overruns, schedule slippage, ESG/carbon metrics, and executive recovery actions. It should be described as an evidence-backed project intelligence workflow, not as a source of unverifiable client outcomes or unsupported performance claims.

## Blog Publishing Policy

Weekly blog articles should include a title, meta description, canonical URL, H1, short answer, practical checklist, example table, FAQ, internal links, and founder/expert attribution. Do not add legal advice, unverifiable performance claims, client claims, accuracy percentages, or regulatory statements that have not been verified. Standards and regulations should be marked "to be verified before publication" unless source evidence is already documented.

## Synthetic Demo Summary

Public demo page: https://www.constrovet.com/demo

The public demo uses the labelled synthetic project "Synthetic Mid-Rise Commercial Building Project." Synthetic figures shown for explanation are: baseline budget ₹12.50 crore, detected leakage exposure ₹38.75 lakh, schedule delay 21 days, ESG findings 4, and documents reviewed 8. These are not client results, audited savings, accuracy claims, testimonials, or proof of recovery.
